div1.entryFree: Parle, subst. parley, conversation, conference (with a view to come to an agreement): the gentlemen that every day with p. encounter me, Gent. I, 2, 5. Chiefly a conference with enemies tending to restore peace: the nature of our quarrel yet never brooked p. Shr. I, 1, 117. our trumpet called you to this gentle p. John II, 205. vouchsafe a p. John II, 205 sound so base a p. R2 I, 1, 192. send the breath of p. into his ruined ears, III, 3, 33 (Qq parlee). this is the latest p. we will admit, H5 III, 3, 2. sound a p. H6C V, 1, 16. break the p. Tit. V, 3, 19. in an angry p. Hml. I, 1, 62.
